title: Enthalpies of solution and hydration
summary: 9701 A Level — ΔH_sol, ΔH_hyd cycles linking lattice energy to solubility with PhET energy sim.
learningObjectives: Define enthalpy change of solution and hydration.
Construct energy cycles relating lattice and hydration enthalpies.
Predict relative solubility from enthalpy data.
Explain the role of entropy where relevant.

simpleExplanation.title: Dissolving ionic solids
simpleExplanation.summary: Dissolution requires breaking the lattice (endothermic) and hydrating ions (exothermic). The balance determines ΔH_sol and influences solubility.
steps: Hydration ∝ charge²/ionic radius — small 2+ ions very exothermic.
Some insoluble salts have endothermic ΔH_sol but entropy-driven solubility.

sections.content: **Enthalpies of solution** (23.2) extend **Born–Haber** (23.1). Draw **complete cycles** with **gaseous ions** — a common source of lost marks.

sections.label: Mark an enthalpy of solution question
